summary: The thickness of objects affects how light passes through them, impacting the radiographic image’s darkness; similarly, adjusting x-ray qualities improves imaging but doesn't involve changing electron velocities post-thermal threshold for most current systems. Thus, varying electron speeds isn’t crucial for typical diagnostic X-rays. \boxed{\text{Electron speed}}
